channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50 sources.

item.title: The effect of exercise on clinical pain: a systematic umbrella review and meta-meta-analysis
item.description: An umbrella review and meta-meta-analysis synthesized evidence from 11 databases, confirming the consistent and significant analgesic effects of exercise across various pain conditions and exercise modalities.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The effectiveness of cardiovascular rehabilitation for transient ischaemic attack and mild stroke: A randomised controlled trial.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial of 140 TIA and mild stroke participants found that a 6-week cardiovascular rehabilitation program significantly improved functional exercise capacity, physical activity, anthropometrics, blood pressure, and quality-of-life over 6 months compared with usual care.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Prevalence of eating disorders and disordered eating in athletes: a systematic review and meta-analysis
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis estimated the prevalence of eating disorders and disordered eating among athletes across training levels and assessment methods, identifying moderators associated with prevalence estimates by searching PubMed/MEDLINE, Scopus, Web of Science, and SportDiscus up to December 2024.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Patient Engagement, Clinical Outcomes, and Satisfaction of an Advanced Orthopedic Virtual Physical Therapy Model: A Retrospective Cohort Study.
item.description: A retrospective cohort study found that an advanced orthopedic virtual physical therapy model achieved high patient engagement and satisfaction, with 70.9% of referred patients completing full care episodes (exceeding national benchmarks of 43%) and 96% achieving Minimal Clinically Important Differences.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Pelvic floor muscle training for pelvic organ prolapse: A systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials confirmed the efficacy of pelvic floor muscle training for pelvic organ prolapse, demonstrating significant improvements in prolapse symptoms and quality of life.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: APTA Report Finds Demand for Pelvic Health Physical Therapy Outpaces Access
item.description: A new APTA report, "APTA State of Pelvic Health Physical Therapy," reveals that the demand for pelvic health physical therapy in the U.S. significantly outpaces access, highlighting workforce, access, and awareness challenges.
item.author: APTA

item.title: Effectiveness of non-pharmacological interventions for lower limb motor impairment in post-stroke hemiplegia: a systematic review and Bayesian network meta-analysis | Journal of NeuroEngineering and Rehabilitation
item.description: A systematic review and Bayesian network meta-analysis evaluated the effectiveness of non-pharmacological interventions for lower limb motor impairment in post-stroke hemiplegia, identifying key effective modalities.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Pioneering programme launched to test breakthrough spinal treatment in UK rehab centres
item.description: A pioneering program has launched in UK rehab centers to test the real-world impact of Onward Medical's ARC-EX system, a UK-approved spinal cord injury treatment combining targeted electrical spinal cord stimulation with rehabilitation exercises.
item.author: Spinal Surgery News

item.title: Subtype-specific optimal cut-off values of the Berg Balance Scale for predicting independent walking in inpatient stroke rehabilitation: a multicentre cohort study.
item.description: A multicenter retrospective cohort study of 565 stroke patients (316 ischemic, 249 hemorrhagic) in Korea determined subtype-specific optimal cut-off values of the Berg Balance Scale for predicting independent walking at discharge after inpatient rehabilitation.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Sarcopenia Increases Adjacent Segment Degeneration Risk within 3 Years of Anterior Cervical Discectomy and Fusion.
item.description: An exploratory retrospective cohort study of 333 adult patients who underwent elective anterior cervical discectomy and fusion (ACDF) from 2012-2022 found that sarcopenia increases the risk of radiographic adjacent segment degeneration development within 3 years.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Multi-Domain Benefits of Exergaming for Sarcopenia Management in Institutionalized Older Adults: Physical, Psychological, and Social Outcomes.
item.description: A pragmatic quasi-experimental study involving 72 institutionalized older adults with sarcopenia in Taiwan found that a 12-week exergaming intervention (Nintendo Switch Ring Fit Adventure) produced concurrent improvements across physical, psychological, and social domains compared to a control group.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Mental health in tennis: a cross-organisational hybrid summit-Delphi consensus process
item.description: Recommendations for mental healthcare and mental wellness promotion among elite tennis athletes, including juniors, were generated through a multi-method approach involving an in-person summit and an asynchronous Delphi consensus process with 83 participants from governing bodies and the broader tennis ecosystem.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Incidence of injury and illness at the Paris 2024 Paralympic Games: post-pandemic trends and sport-specific risks
item.description: A prospective surveillance study monitored 4450 athletes during the 14-day Paris 2024 Paralympic Games, reporting 535 injuries (prevalence 10.9%) and an incidence of 8.6 injuries per 1000 athlete days, with Taekwondo and football 5-a-side having the highest incidences.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: GaitEncoder: A Foundation Model of Gait Kinematics for Diverse Clinical Applications and Pathologies
item.description: GaitEncoder, a generative foundation model of gait kinematics, was developed using eight gait datasets (65,000+ videos) and demonstrated potential for diverse clinical applications and pathologies, enabling actionable insights from smartphone-based gait analysis.
item.author: medRxiv

item.title: Prehabilitation Improves Surgical Outcomes in Older Adults Undergoing Spinal Fusion: Study
item.description: A randomized clinical trial found that a multimodal prehabilitation program before spinal fusion surgery in older adults was associated with a lower risk of postoperative complications and a shorter hospital stay, but the control group demonstrated better physiological function at 90-day follow-up.
item.author: Medical Dialogues

item.title: Immediate Weight-Bearing and Early Ambulation Rehabilitation After Femoral Neck System Fixation for Undisplaced Femoral Neck Fractures: A Prospective Randomized Controlled Trial
item.description: A prospective randomized controlled trial found that immediate weight-bearing and early ambulation rehabilitation after femoral neck system fixation for undisplaced femoral neck fractures was safe and effective, promoting earlier functional recovery.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: The Impact of a Single Bout of Antagonist Stretching on Agonist Muscle Performance and Range of Motion: A Systematic review and Meta-analysis
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis evaluated the impact of a single bout of antagonist stretching on agonist muscle performance and range of motion, aiming to resolve conflicting findings in the literature.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Validity and reliability of remote administration of the one-leg stand, timed up and go and 30-second sit-to-stand via video call in patients with lumbar spinal stenosis awaiting decompression surgery
item.description: A study validated the reliability of remote administration of the one-leg stand, timed up and go, and 30-second sit-to-stand tests via video call in patients with lumbar spinal stenosis, showing strong agreement with in-person assessments.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Cost-effectiveness of Pilates compared with home-based exercises for people with chronic non-specific low back pain: a trial-based economic evaluation.
item.description: A trial-based economic evaluation found that a Pilates exercise program showed a more favorable cost-effectiveness ratio compared to home-based exercises for individuals with chronic non-specific low back pain in Brazil, though specific effect sizes were not detailed in the abstract.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Acute Modulation of Lumbar Motor Unit Behavior During Experimentally Induced Low Back Pain: A Motor Unit Decomposition Analysis.
item.description: This study investigated the effects of experimentally induced acute low back pain on force steadiness and motor unit discharge characteristics in 33 healthy participants, revealing specific neuromuscular adaptations during a sustained spinal extension task.
item.author: PubMed
